topic.name: Child Protection and Education Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting vulnerable children and improving educational standards and outcomes.

Document 1: Preventing Sex Trafficking and Strengthening Families Act - Title I: Protecting Children and Youth At Risk of Sex Trafficking

require the state plan for foster care and adoption assistance to demonstrate that the state agency has developed policies and procedures for identifying, documenting in agency records, and determining appropriate services with respect to, any child or youth...
Document 2: Elementary and Secondary Education Reauthorization Act of 2011 - Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Maintains the requirement conditioning a state's receipt of funds under part A on its developing and implementing academic content and achievement standards...
Document 3: Troop Talent Act of 2013 - Directs the Secretaries of the military departments... to make information on civilian credentialing opportunities available to members of the Armed Forces
Requires the information made available to be consistent with the Transition Goals Plans Success program...

topic.name: Tariff and Public Health Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments related to tariff suspensions and public health funding adjustments.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on phosphonic acid, maleic anhydride sodium salt complex.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the suspension of duty on the mixture of various ammonium salts.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to adjust the minimum state allotments for Projects for Assistance in Transition from Homelessness programs.

topic.name: Regulatory and Electoral Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the integrity and accountability of government officials and the electoral process.

Document 1: Requires the basic pay of any federal agency official who is responsible for the issuance of regulations to be held in escrow
if such official fails to issue such regulations by the date specified by law.
Document 2: Imposes a five-year ban on communications by a former political appointee with the intent to influence officers or employees
imposes a lifetime ban on lobbying by a former political appointee on behalf of a foreign government.
Document 3: Prohibit the appropriate state or local election official from providing a ballot for an election for federal office to an individual unless the individual presents a current, valid, state-issued photo identification
Requires each state to conduct regular tests of the equipment used to tabulate votes in voting systems.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to the temporary suspension of duties on various goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Phosphoric acid, tris (2-ethylhexyl) ester.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain printed golf umbrellas.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain clock movements.

topic.name: Surveillance and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures aimed at regulating surveillance practices and protecting individual privacy rights in the context of government intelligence and law enforcement activities.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to exclude call data records from the categories of tangible things...
Authorizes the FBI, without a court order, to direct a telecommunications carrier to search call data records...
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit intentionally...intercepting geolocation information pertaining to another person;
Prohibits a person providing covered services from intentionally divulging geolocation information pertaining to another person...
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make available to Congress and the public all records of individual payments made since January 1, 2003...
Requires such records...to be maintained on a publicly accessible Internet website.

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments related to Medicare, specifically addressing coverage, payment adjustments, and service provisions.

Document 1: Medicare Information Act of 2011
direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to provide to each eligible individual annually a statement of Medicare part A contributions and benefits.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
requires hospice programs to report comprehensive data on hospice care.
Document 3: Medicare Adult Day Care Services Act of 2009
provide for Medicare coverage of substitute adult day care services.

topic.name: Home and Community-Based Services
topic.description: The documents discuss legislation aimed at improving access to home and community-based services, health plans, and employment opportunities for individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: establish a single-entry point system of grants to states for their own single-entry point systems providing information and counseling about public and private long-term services and supports
enhanced nursing home diversion programs enabling individuals ineligible for medical assistance under SSA title XIX (Medicaid) to avoid admission into nursing homes
Document 2: establishment and governance of association health plans (AHPs)
providing health insurance coverage in connection with a certified AHP
Document 3: a Medicaid Buy-In State meeting certain criteria may receive bonus payments for expanding individual integrated employment
reducing subminimum wage work, congregate setting work, or facility-based day habilitation placements for working-age individuals with a disability

topic.name: Regulatory Amendments in Health and Environment
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative amendments aimed at regulating health-related substances and environmental standards.

Document 1: This bill amends the Controlled Substances Act to allow a pharmacist to partially fill a prescription for a schedule II controlled substance.
a pharmacist may partially fill a prescription for a schedule II controlled substance in other circumstances in accordance with existing Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) regulations.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to delay the effective date of reclassification of a nonattainment area to a higher classification of nonattainment for failing to meet national ambient air quality standards.

the state in which the area is located submits a claim to the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) that the area would have attained the relevant standard by the attainment date but for emissions emanating from outside the United States.
Document 3: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to require as a condition of each license issued for a commercial or industrial nuclear production or utilization facility that, in the case of an unplanned release described in this Act, the licensee notify, within 24 hours after the release, the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC).
Requires the NRC to establish on its website a database of all notifications it receives of such unplanned releases.

topic.name: Environmental and Agricultural Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative actions related to environmental regulation, sustainable development, and agricultural policy reforms.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing regulations under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act that would adversely impact employment in coal mines.
Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit regulation concerning greenhouse gas emissions.
Document 2: Establishes an Office of Sustainable Housing and Communities to promote energy-efficient housing.
Requires the Interagency Council on Sustainable Communities to ensure coordination of federal policy on sustainable development.
Document 3: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to notify farm owners regarding their opportunity to elect to retain base acres.

topic.name: Immigration and Human Rights Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing relief and protections for specific groups of immigrants and addressing discrimination in employment based on identity.

Document 1: Development, Relief, and Education for Alien Minors Act of 2010
Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional nonimmigrant status, an alien who entered the United States before his or her 16th birthday.
Document 2: September 11 Family Humanitarian Relief and Patriotism Act

Provides permanent resident status adjustment for a requesting alien who was on September 10, 2001, the wife, child, or dependent son or daughter of a lawful nonimmigrant alien who died as a result of the September 11, 2001, terrorist attacks.
Document 3: Employment Non-Discrimination Act of 2011
Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.

topic.name: Federal Agency Authorizations and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the authorization, oversight, and operational efficiency of federal agencies, including the Coast Guard and small business programs.

Document 1: authorizes appropriations for the Coast Guard for FY2016-FY2017
The Coast Guard shall develop and implement a plan to conduct every two years a multirater assessment
Document 2: reauthorizes through FY2022 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR)
directs the SBA to maintain an Office of Technology to carry out oversight
Document 3: requires executive agencies to ensure that inherently governmental functions are performed by federal employees
requires each agency head to submit to the Director of the Office of Management and Budget an annual report on service contracts

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures and acts aimed at providing foreign assistance, imposing sanctions, and promoting democracy and recovery in various countries.

Document 1: Prohibits specified U.S. assistance to a country that opposes the U.S. position in the United Nations.
Authorizes the Secretary of State to exempt a country from such prohibition if...
Document 2: This bill authorizes the President to provide technical and financial assistance for programs that...
support democracy programs that promote political reforms, including strengthening democratic institutions and rule of law.
Document 3: States that U.S. sanctions, controls, and regulations relating to Syria shall remain in effect until...
Directs the President to provide assistance to support a democratic transition in Syria.

topic.name: Tax Policy and Retirement Plans
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at modifying tax codes related to retirement plans and individual taxpayer benefits.

Document 1: require certain employers who do not maintain qualified retirement plans to make available to their eligible employees a payroll deposit individual retirement account (IRA) arrangement
impose a penalty on employers who fail to provide eligible employees access to automatic IRA arrangements.
Document 2: allow individual taxpayers with adjusted gross incomes of less than $75,000 a tax credit for 10% of their income tax
eliminate income taxes for individual taxpayers with adjusted gross incomes of less than $12,500.
Document 3: deny employers a tax deduction for payments of excessive compensation to any full-time employee
require such employers to file a report with the Secretary of the Treasury on excessive compensation.

topic.name: Legislation on Social Issues
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures addressing social issues such as health care, territorial representation, and substance abuse treatment.

Document 1: Prohibits funds appropriated for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) from being used to pay for an abortion
in a case where a pregnant detainee suffers from a physical disorder, injury, or illness that would place the detainee in danger of death unless an abortion is performed
Document 2: Permits statues honoring deceased citizens of U.S. territories to be placed in Statuary Hall of the U.S. Capitol
Limits such statues to one per jurisdiction
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to include territories and Indian tribes as eligible grant recipients
address the manufacture, sale, and use of methamphetamine

topic.name: Federal Grants for Innovation and Support
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various federal initiatives aimed at providing grants to improve technology, education, and support services for specific populations.

Document 1: Directs the Attorney General to make grants to states and local governments to purchase or improve forensic DNA technology.
require grants under the DNA identification program to pay the salaries of employees of DNA criminal laboratories.
Document 2: establishes the Alyce Spotted Bear and Walter Soboleff Commission on Native Children in the Office of Tribal Justice of the Department of Justice.
must conduct a comprehensive study of federal, state, local, and tribal programs that serve Native children.
Document 3: requires the Department of Education to award grants, on a competitive basis, to state educational agencies to establish or expand the number of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics, including computer science (STEM) secondary schools.
The NSF Partnerships for Innovation Program shall administer a Technology Commercialization Awards Pilot Program.

topic.name: Electric Grid and Renewable Energy Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative efforts to enhance electric grid infrastructure and promote renewable energy initiatives.

Document 1: requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to establish a grants program for eligible partnerships to develop and implement projects related to achieving the transformation of the future electric grid
using a comprehensive approach to electric system design and architecture.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish an Energy and Environment Block Grant Program to make block grants to state or local governments for: (1) energy efficiency and climate protection strategy

requires a local government unit that is a grant recipient to submit to the Secretary a proposed Energy Efficiency and Climate Protection Strategy establishing goals for increased energy efficiency and reduction of greenhouse gas emissions.
Document 3: allow a 30% tax credit for investment in a qualifying offshore wind facility
direct the Secretary of the Treasury to establish a qualifying credit for offshore wind facilities program to consider and award certifications for investments eligible for such a credit.

topic.name: Small Business Financial Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing financial resources and protections for small businesses and entrepreneurs.

Document 1: authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to guarantee the payment of up to $4 billion per fiscal year for debentures
expresses the sense of Congress that SBICs would benefit from partnerships with community banks
Document 2: require the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to extend the terms of marketing assistance loans
suspends the accrual of interest on the loan during the extension period
Document 3: extends credit card protections under such Act to small businesses
prohibits a creditor from discriminating against any business concern having 50 or fewer employees

topic.name: Land Exchange Legislation
topic.description: Legislation concerning the exchange and conveyance of land between federal and non-federal entities.

Document 1: Requires that approximately 25,977 acres of land in Nevada...be held in trust for the benefit of the Duckwater Shoshone Tribe
Prohibits class II or class III gaming on such land.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA), if the White Lotus Foundation...offers to convey to the Secretary a parcel of non-federal land
Subjects the land exchange to provisions under the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA), if the Sugar Loaf Fire Protection District...offers to convey non-federal land
Applies provisions of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 relating to the exchange of public lands.

topic.name: Educational Policy and Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving educational standards, access to resources, and financial support for students and institutions in the U.S.

Document 1: establishing, improving, or expanding Chinese language and cultural studies instruction
increasing the number of students who receive substantial exposure to Chinese language and cultural studies
Document 2: require states...to maintain their support for institutions of higher education
providing need-based grants to students attending IHEs or participating in study abroad programs
Document 3: require states to adopt student achievement standards that lead to college and career readiness
ensures that the state's public school system enables students to meet those standards

topic.name: Housing and Financial Counseling Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving housing access and financial counseling for consumers, particularly in relation to mortgage practices and student loans.

Document 1: Fair Mortgage Practices Act of 2007 - Prohibits any person... from engaging in the business of loan origination without first obtaining...
Expanding Housing Opportunities Through Education and Counseling Act - Amends the Department of Housing and Urban Development Act to...
Document 2: Rural Housing Preservation Act of 2016 - This bill amends the Housing Act of 1949 to direct the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to extend rural housing vouchers...
Document 3: Empowering Students Through Enhanced Financial Counseling Act - This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify loan counseling requirements...

topic.name: Governmental Authorizations and Studies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on governmental actions related to infrastructure funding, memorial designations, and agricultural studies impacting food safety and trade.

Document 1: Authorizes the International Boundary and Water Commission to reimburse state and local governments for expenses incurred in designing, constructing, and rehabilitating Commission water projects.
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1048 West Robinhood Drive in Stockton, California, as the 'W. Ronald Coale Memorial Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to conduct studies on the economic and trade impact of exports of food products from cloned animals.

topic.name: Veterans Retirement and Disability Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the retirement and disability benefits of members of the Armed Forces and veterans.

Document 1: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2008 to back-date to September 11, 2001, the period of active duty or performed active service
members of Ready Reserve components of the Armed Forces shall receive credit in determining eligibility for early receipt of non-regular service retired pay.
Document 2: Adds new federal provisions concerning the retirement for disability of members of the Armed Forces

Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to continue after discharge or separation from active duty respite care and aid and other extended care benefits for members who incur a serious injury or illness while serving on such duty.
Document 3: requires the Federal Retirement Thrift Investment Board to prescribe regulations to permit continued participation in the Thrift Savings Plan
monthly veterans disability compensation shall be deemed to be his or her basic pay for a monthly pay period

topic.name: Healthcare and Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to healthcare transparency and criminal justice reform, particularly concerning drug offenses and penalties.

Document 1: Prohibits any person from making any deceptive or misleading statement...
Requires any person who is advertising health care services...
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to reduce penalties...
Repeals a provision providing for up to three years' imprisonment...
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to increase or impose new criminal penalties...
Expands criminal forfeiture provisions to include certain crimes involving terrorism...

topic.name: Water Ecosystem Restoration Acts
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the protection and restoration of major U.S. water ecosystems, including the Everglades, Great Lakes, and Chesapeake Bay.

Document 1: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 2000 to authorize projects under the Comprehensive Everglades Restoration Plan
projects identified in the Plan for which a project implementation report is completed
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to include goals of the Great Lakes Restoration Initiative
requires the establishment of the Great Lakes Advisory Board for restoration and protection matters
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to revise definitions under the Chesapeake Bay Program
requires the Administrator of the EPA to make public a Chesapeake Bay action plan to protect and restore the Chesapeake Bay

topic.name: Tax Credit Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts that amend the Internal Revenue Code to provide tax credits for specific groups or activities.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an income-based tax credit
for legal expenses paid to establish a legal guardianship of a disabled individual.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain small business owners
a refundable tax credit for increasing their payrolls in 2010 and 2011 by specified percentages.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal shipping investment withdrawal tax rules
and allow U.S. corporate shareholders a tax deduction for dividends.
